The caller must be the current authority."},"setPolicy(bytes32,uint256)":{"details":"Restricted: only the governance authority (via AccessManager) may call this.","params":{"key":"Policy identifier.","value":"The policy value to store."}}},"title":"GovernancePolicyStore","version":1},"userdoc":{"events":{"PolicyRemoved(bytes32)":{"notice":"Emitted when a policy is removed (reset to inactive)."},"PolicySet(bytes32,uint256)":{"notice":"Emitted when a policy value is set or updated."}},"kind":"user","methods":{"getPolicy(bytes32)":{"notice":"Returns the current value for a policy key."},"getPolicyOrRevert(bytes32)":{"notice":"Returns the policy value only if it is active; reverts otherwise."},"policyActive(bytes32)":{"notice":"Returns whether a policy has been explicitly set."},"removePolicy(bytes32)":{"notice":"Remove a policy (mark it as inactive)."},"setPolicy(bytes32,uint256)":{"notice":"Set or update a policy value."}},"notice":"Stores governance-managed risk policy overrides that can be read by RiskGateway. Risk parameters in RiskGateway are immutable by design (set at constructor time) to prevent runtime manipulation. GovernancePolicyStore provides a separate governance- controlled layer for policy overrides that the gateway can optionally consult. Each policy is identified by a bytes32 key and stores an arbitrary uint256 value. The governance body (via AccessManager) is the sole authorized 1b9bb4a0ea47d96b53c13ab9a6e4270a518ea395b1424092fd74dc","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://d806b0c87fbcaf0cb827b51203b27f8289b13743aeadb5867fbbe041fcf87a92","dweb:/ipfs/QmfTEGiDGxKDaqBuBJ5wWJockGDrsEBZnW5S7UWrKhAxGc"]},"node_modules/@openzeppelin/contracts/utils/Context.sol":{"keccak256":"0x493033a8d1b176a037b2cc6a04dad01a5c157722049bbecf632ca876224dd4b2","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://6a708e8a5bdb1011c2c381c9a5cfd8a9a956d7d0a9dc1bd8bcdaf52f76ef2f12","dweb:/ipfs/Qmax9WHBnVsZP46ZxEMNRQpLQnrdE4dK8LehML1Py8FowF"]}},"version":1}

params.authority_: The AccessManager that governs this contract.

returns.value: The stored policy value (0 if not set or if set to 0).

getPolicyOrRevert(bytes32).details: Useful for callers that need to distinguish "not set" from "set to 0".

isConsumingScheduledOp().details: Returns true only in the context of a delayed restricted call, at the moment that the scheduled operation is being consumed. Prevents denial of service for delayed restricted calls in the case that the contract performs attacker controlled calls.

returns._0: True if the policy is active (has been set and not removed).

removePolicy(bytes32).details: Restricted: only the governance authority (via AccessManager) may call this.

setAuthority(address).details: Transfers control to a new authority. The caller must be the current authority.

setPolicy(bytes32,uint256).details: Restricted: only the governance authority (via AccessManager) may call this.

getPolicy(bytes32).notice: Returns the current value for a policy key.

getPolicyOrRevert(bytes32).notice: Returns the policy value only if it is active; reverts otherwise.

policyActive(bytes32).notice: Returns whether a policy has been explicitly set.

removePolicy(bytes32).notice: Remove a policy (mark it as inactive).

setPolicy(bytes32,uint256).notice: Set or update a policy value.
